name: Free Online Security Services
description: Defensive-only curated tree generated automatically from the D3fD3c0y/Free-online-security-services repository. Some explicitly offensive or malware-download categories from the source are intentionally excluded.

children.name: AIGregator
children.description: Find the right AI tool, fast. Search, compare, and bookmark across every AI category.

children.name: Claude-OSINT
children.description: Two paired Claude skills · 90+ recon modules · 48 secret-regex patterns · 80+ dorks · 9 read-only credential validators · 27 attack-path templates · 5,500+ lines of structured tradecraft. Drop-in SKILL.md files that turn Claude into a god-mode external recon operator for authorized red-team and bug-bounty engagements.

children.name: PromptIntel
children.description: A collaborative threat intel platform to identify patterns and artifacts indicating potential exploitation or misuse of Large Language Models.

children.name: Archive.today
children.description: Archive.today is a time capsule for web pages! It takes a 'snapshot' of a webpage that will always be online even if the original page disappears. It saves a text and a graphical copy of the page for better accuracy and provides a short and reliable link to an unalterable record of any web page including those from Web 2.0 sites.

children.name: Software Heritage
children.description: We collect and preserve software in source code form, because software embodies our technical and scientific knowledge and humanity cannot afford the risk of losing it.

children.name: Wayback Machine
children.description: The Internet Archive, a 501(c)(3) non-profit, is building a digital library of Internet sites and other cultural artifacts in digital form. Like a paper library, we provide free access to researchers, historians, scholars, people with print disabilities, and the general public. Our mission is to provide Universal Access to All Knowledge.

children.name: Shodan Search modifiers
children.description: There are a set of special terms that you can use to refine your search results.

children.name: VirusTotal Search modifiers
children.description: There are a set of special terms that you can use to refine your search results.

children.name: AnyRun
children.description: Interactive online sandbox. Also perform sample download from URL. Use the corporate account.

children.name: Cape
children.description: Context's cuckoo sandbox. Also perform sample download from URL.

children.name: Hybrid-Analysis
children.description: CrowdStrike's public sandbox. Also perform sample download from URL. Free account required to download samples.

children.name: Joe Sandbox
children.description: a comprehensive, cloud-based malware and phishing analysis platform developed by Joe Security.

children.name: MalShare
children.description: The MalShare Project is a collaborative effort to create a community driven public malware repository that works to build additional tools to benefit the security community at large.

children.name: MalwareBazaar Database
children.description: MalwareBazaar is a project from abuse.ch with the goal of sharing malware samples with the infosec community, AV vendors and threat intelligence providers.

children.name: Polyswarm
children.description: Crowdsourced Malware Analysis for new and emerging threats. Free and premium services.

children.name: VX-underground
children.description: A free Malware repository providing researchers access to samples.

children.name: VirusShare
children.description: A free Malware repository providing researchers access to samples.

children.name: VirusTotal
children.description: Analyse suspicious files, domains, IPs and URLs to detect malware and other breaches, automatically share them with the security community. Premium accoutn required to downlaod malware sample

children.name: CyberChief
children.description: UK GCHQ's free online tool to convert, parse or carry out well over 100 different operations.

children.name: UnCoder
children.description: online translator for SIEM saved searches, filters, queries, API requests, correlation and Sigma rules to help SOC Analysts, Threat Hunters and SIEM Engineers.

children.name: Anthropic LLM ATT&CK Navigator
children.description: Companion to Mapping AI-Enabled Cyber Threats mapped to MITRE ATT&CK v18 and scored on the AI Risk Enablement Score (ARiES).

children.name: CIS Critical Security Controls
children.description: The CIS Critical Security Controls (CIS Controls) are a prioritized set of CIS Safeguards to defend against the most prevalent cyber attacks against systems and networks. They are mapped to and referenced by multiple legal, regulatory, and policy frameworks. PDF version at https://learn.cisecurity.org/cis-controls-v8-1-guide-pdf

children.name: CISA Zero Trust Maturity Model
children.description: The Zero Trust Maturity Model is one of many roadmaps for agencies to reference as they transition towards a zero trust architecture. PDF version at https://www.cisa.gov/sites/default/files/2023-04/CISA_Zero_Trust_Maturity_Model_Version_2_508c.pdf

children.name: CSA Cloud Controls Matrix (CCM)
children.description: The Cloud Controls Matrix (CCM) is a cybersecurity control framework made up of 207 controls across 17 security domains. The CCM maps to industry best practices and is considered the standard for cloud security and privacy. The accompanying Consensus Assessment Initiative Questionnaire (CAIQ) provides a set of “yes/no” questions to assess security controls. PDF version at https://cloudsecurityalliance.org/download/artifacts/cloud-controls-matrix-v4-1

children.name: Cybersecurity Capability Maturity Model (C2M2)
children.description: The Cybersecurity Capability Maturity Model (C2M2) is a free tool to help organizations evaluate their cybersecurity capabilities and optimize security investments. It uses a set of industry-vetted cybersecurity practices focused on both information technology (IT) and operations technology (OT) assets and environments. PDF version at https://www.energy.gov/sites/default/files/2021-07/C2M2%20Version%202.0%20July%202021_508.pdf

children.name: Cybersecurity Maturity Model Certification (CMMC) 2.0)
children.description: CMMC 2.0 is the U.S. Department of Defense (DoD) framework designed to protect defense supply chain data. It requires defense contractors and subcontractors to implement robust cybersecurity practices to safeguard Controlled Unclassified Information (CUI) and Federal Contract Information (FCI). PDF version at https://dodcio.defense.gov/Portals/0/Documents/CMMC/ModelOverview_V2.0_FINAL2_20211202_508.pdf

children.name: DISARM Red Framework
children.description: The DISARM Red Framework provides a common language for documenting influence operations. When different organisations describe what they're seeing in the same terms, they can share intelligence, and gain a better understanding of actors' manipulative behaviours.

children.name: Essential Eight Maturity Model
children.description: The Essential Eight Maturity Model is a prioritized cybersecurity framework developed by the Australian Signals Directorate (ASD) to protect internet-connected IT networks. It breaks down eight foundational mitigation strategies into four distinct maturity levels to help organizations benchmark and improve their cyber resilience. PDF version at https://www.cyber.gov.au/sites/default/files/2023-03/PROTECT%20-%20Essential%20Eight%20Maturity%20Model%20%28November%202022%29.pdf

children.name: Mitre Att&ck Enterprise Matrix
children.description: The tactics and techniques representing the MITRE ATT&CK® Matrix for Enterprise.

children.name: MITRE ATTACK Flow
children.description: With Attack Flow, you will capture the entire attack and communicate what matters

children.name: MITRE ATTACK Flow Builder
children.description: Interactive interface of the MITRE ATTACK Flow

children.name: Mitre Att&ck Navigator
children.description: Interactive interface of the MITRE ATT&CK Framework which can be use as graphical reference for understanding/following invetsigation.

children.name: Mitre CAPEC
children.description: CAPEC™ provide a comprehensive dictionary of known patterns of attack employed by adversaries to exploit known weaknesses in cyber-enabled capabilities.

children.name: Mitre CREF Navigator
children.description: The CREF Navigator™ was developed as a web based relational tool distilling the complex concepts and relationships from NIST SP 800-160 Volume 2 (Rev 1) into useful cyber resiliency terms, tables, and relationship visualizations enabling architectural and engineering analysis.

children.name: Mitre CVE
children.description: The mission of the CVE® Program is to identify, define, and catalog publicly disclosed cybersecurity vulnerabilities.

children.name: Mitre CWE
children.description: Common Weakness Enumeration (CWE™) is a community-developed list of common software and hardware weaknesses. A “weakness” is a condition in a software, firmware, hardware, or service component that, under certain circumstances, could contribute to the introduction of vulnerabilities.

children.name: Mitre DEFEND
children.description: A framework in which we encode a countermeasure knowledge base, but more specifically, a knowledge graph.

children.name: Mitre EMB3D
children.description: The EMB3D Threat Model provides a cultivated knowledge base of cyber threats to embedded devices, providing a common understanding of these threats with security mechanisms to mitigate them.

children.name: NCSC Cyber Assessment Framework
children.description: TThe CAF is a collection of cyber security guidance for organisations that play a vital role in the day-to-day life of the UK, with a focus on essential functions. PDF version at https://www.ncsc.gov.uk/sites/default/files/documents/NCSC-Cyber-Assessment-Framework-4.0.pdf

children.name: NIST Cybersecurity Framework 2.0
children.description: The Cybersecurity Framework (CSF) 2.0 is designed to help organizations of all sizes and sectors — including industry, government, academia, and nonprofit — to manage and reduce their cybersecurity risks. It is useful regardless of the maturity level and technical sophistication of an organization’s cybersecurity programs. PDF version at https://nvlpubs.nist.gov/nistpubs/SpecialPublications/NIST.SP.800-37r2.pdf

children.name: NIST Risk Management Framework (SP 800-37 Rev. 2)
children.description: The NIST Risk Management Framework (RMF SP 800-37 Rev. 2) is a comprehensive, flexible, and repeatable 7-step process developed by the National Institute of Standards and Technology. It integrates security, privacy, and cyber supply chain risk management into the system development life cycle, allowing organizations to custom-fit cybersecurity practices to their specific needs. PDF version at https://nvlpubs.nist.gov/nistpubs/CSWP/NIST.CSWP.29.pdf

children.name: NIST Security and Privacy Controls Framework (SP 800-53 Rev. 5)
children.description: The NIST Security and Privacy Controls Framework is a foundational catalog of security and privacy controls developed by the National Institute of Standards and Technology (NIST). It provides a comprehensive, customizable framework to help organizations protect their information systems and data from a wide range of threats. PDF version at https://nvlpubs.nist.gov/nistpubs/SpecialPublications/NIST.SP.800-53r5.pdf

children.name: OSWAP Application Security Verification Standard
children.description: The OWASP Application Security Verification Standard (ASVS) Project provides a basis for testing web application technical security controls and also provides developers with a list of requirements for secure development. PDF version at https://github.com/OWASP/ASVS/raw/v5.0.0/5.0/OWASP_Application_Security_Verification_Standard_5.0.0_en.pdf

children.name: OSWAP BLADE
children.description: The OWASP Business Logic Attack Definition (BLADE) Framework, is an open-source knowledge-base created to help cybersecurity professionals identify the phases, tactics and techniques used by adversaries to exploit weaknesses in the business logic of web facing systems (websites and APIs)

children.name: OSWAP Top 10
children.description: The OWASP Top 10 is the latest industry awareness document highlighting the most critical web application vulnerabilities. It reflects an evolution in how modern software is built and attacked, introducing new categories for supply chain and error-handling flaws

children.name: Secure Design and Development Cybersecurity Capability Maturity Model (SD2-C2M2)
children.description: TThe Secure Design and Development Cybersecurity Capability Maturity Model (SD2-C2M2) is a specialized assessment tool developed by the U.S. Department of Energy (DOE) and Pacific Northwest National Laboratory (PNNL). It helps hardware/software developers and integrators measure and improve the cybersecurity maturity of their design and development lifecycles. PDF version at https://sd2-c2m2.pnnl.gov/documents/SD2-C2M2_Next-Generation_Cyber_Resilience_by_Design.pdf

children.name: C2IntelFeed
children.description: Free and Open Source Threat Intelligence Feeds dedicated to Cobalt Strike C2, Empire C2 and PoshC2.

children.name: Covert Threat Intelligence Feed list
children.description: Free and Open Source Threat Intelligence Feeds.

children.description: Analyse all available security IP Feeds, mainly related to on-line attacks, on-line service abuse, malwares, botnets, command and control servers and other cybercrime activities.

children.description: Free and Open Source Threat Intelligence Feeds.

children.description: Resource collection focused on identifying and understanding malicious hardware and device abuse.

children.name: LOLCerts

children.name: LOLOL

children.name: LOLRMM

children.name: LOOBins

children.name: LOLProx

children.description: Catalogs legitimate domains that attackers abuse for phishing, command and control, exfiltration, and tool downloads.

children.name: LOTP
children.description: Living Off the Pipeline; inventories CI/CD and development tools with lesser-known code execution or workflow injection abuse paths.

children.name: LOTWebhooks
children.description: Living Off The Webhooks project focused on legitimate webhook services and potential abuse scenarios.

children.name: LUTLOL

children.description: Information on Windows persistence mechanisms organized to support protection and detection engineering.

children.description: Concise reference for commonly abused binaries and Living Off The Land techniques.

children.name: WTFBins

It uses adaptive threat analysis technology to detect evasive malware and extract relevant Indicators of Compromise (IOCs). Our threat-agnostic scanning supports a wide variety of files and URLs to identify IOCs swiftly and efficiently.

children.name: ThreatLandscape Browser-based STIX 2.1 bundle visualizer
children.description: Llightweight STIX 2.1 viewer that runs entirely in the browser. No login, no install, just upload a bundle JSON and get an interactive relationship graph.

children.name: ThreatNexus
children.description: Nation-State APT Intelligence

children.name: Cloud Security Alliance Cloud Controls Matrix (CCM)
children.description: The Cloud Controls Matrix (CCM) is a cybersecurity control framework that maps to industry best practices and is considered the standard for cloud security and privacy.

children.name: Confiant Malvertising Attack Matrix
children.description: Detailing threat actors through Malvertising Activity.

children.name: ITM Insider Threat Matrix
children.description: ITM is a continually growing framework for Digital Investigators investigating instances of computer-enabled insider threats in organizations of any size.

children.name: MITRE Atlas (AI)
children.description: Navigate threats to AI systems through real-world insights.

children.name: MITRE Cloud Matrix
children.description: The tactics and techniques representing the MITRE ATT&CK® cloud platforms.

children.name: MITRE Fight Fraud Framework Matrix
children.description: The MITRE Fight Fraud Framework™ (F3) is a curated knowledge base of tactics and techniques used by financial fraud actors, derived from real-world observations of cyber fraud incidents.

children.name: Microsoft Azure Threat Research Matrix
children.description: The purpose of the Azure Threat Research Matrix (ATRM) is to educate readers on the potential of Azure-based tactics, techniques, and procedures (TTPs).

children.name: Microsoft DevOps Threat Matrix
children.description: Our goal for developing the threat matrix for DevOps is to build a comprehensive knowledgebase that defenders can use to keep track of and build defenses against relevant attack techniques.

children.name: Microsoft Threat Matrix for Kubernetes
children.description: Threat Matrix.

children.name: Microsoft Threat Matrix for Storage Services
children.description: The purpose of the threat matrix for storage services is to conceptualize the known tactics, techniques, and procedures (TTP) that adversaries may use against this type accounts.

children.name: OWASP Threat and Safeguard Matrix (TaSM)
children.description: The Threat and Safeguard Matrix (TaSM) is an action-oriented view to safeguard and enable the business created by CISO Tradecraft.

children.name: Own the Browser
children.description: Browser Directory - Explore browser profiles and assessments

children.name: Space Attack Research and Tactic Analysis (SPARTA) matrix
children.description: SPARTA is intended to provide unclassified information to space professionals about how spacecraft may be compromised via cyber and traditional counterspace means.

children.name: AbuseIPDB
children.description: Provide a central blacklist for webmasters, system administrators, and other interested parties to report and find IP addresses that have been associated with malicious activity online.

children.name: Censys
children.description: Uses Internet scan data to give organizations the visibility they need to defend against attacks and improve their overall security hygiene.

children.name: Cisco Talos
children.description: The Talos IP and Domain Reputation Center is the world’s most comprehensive real-time threat detection network.

children.name: DNSDumpster
children.description: Domain research tool that can discover hosts related to a domain.

children.name: DNSlytics
children.description: Find out everything about a domain name, IP address or provider.

children.name: SecurityTrails
children.description: World's largest repository of historical DNS data.

children.name: Google Safe Browsing
children.description: Check site status in Google Safe Browsing database.

children.name: HackerTarget
children.description: Domain research tool that can discover hosts related to a domain.

children.name: IPQS
children.description: Search Accurate IP Details — Check Proxy, VPN, or TOR Activity With Anonymous Proxy & VPN Detection.

children.name: IPVoid
children.description: IP address tools to discover details about IP addresses.

children.name: Ipinfo.io
children.description: The most reliable, accurate, and in-depth source of IP address data available anywhere.

children.name: Lookyloo
children.description: Tool developed by CIRCL (the Luxembourg CERT) that helps to have a quick overview of a website by scraping it and displaying a tree of domains calling each other.

children.name: MultiRBL
children.description: IP check for sending Mailservers.

children.name: Onyphe
children.description: Search engine for open-source and cyber threat intelligence data collected by crawling various sources available on the Internet or by listening to Internet background noise. ONYPHE does correlate this information with data gathered by performing active Internet scanning for connected devices.

children.name: Robtex
children.description: Gather public information about IP numbers, domain names, hostnames. Autonomous systems, routes, etc.

children.name: SSL Blacklist
children.description: List of "bad" SSL certificates identified by abuse.ch to be associated with malware or botnet activities.

children.name: ScreenshotMachine
children.description: Online tool that creates screenshots of websites in a safe way.

children.name: Shodan
children.description: The world's first search engine for Internet-connected devices.

children.name: ThreatMiner
children.description: Free analysis from data collection and provide intelligence analysis.

children.name: URLVoid
children.description: Analyze a website through multiple blacklist engines and online reputation tools.

children.name: URLscan.io
children.description: Analyse websites and the resources they request. it will let you take a look at the individual resources that are requested when a site is visited.

children.name: Validin
children.description: Connect the dots across threat indicators. Validin correlates unrelated IPs, domains, and hashes with organized threat activity, analyzes registration and hosting patterns, and attributes domains and IPs to ongoing APT activity.

children.name: VirusTotal
children.description: Compare URL categorization from multiple URL filtering solutions vendors.

children.name: WebCheck
children.description: We give you X-RayVision for your Website

children.name: ZoomEye
children.description: Cyberspace Search Engine recording information of devices, websites, services and components, etc.

children.name: AWASP Top Ten
children.description: Web application vulnerabilities ranked by real-world exploitation frequency — not theoretical risk. Drawn from breach reports, CVE data, and incident response findings.

children.name: CVE2EPSS
children.description: A simple web app which enables you to quickly and easily lookup EPSS scores for a CVE ID.

children.name: CVEdetails.com
children.description: CVEdetails.com offers a complete CVE database enhanced with additional information including advisories, exploits, tools, source code changes and much more.

children.name: Exploit Database
children.description: Archive of Exploits, Shellcode and security papers.

children.name: National Vulnerability Database (NVD)
children.description: A collection of vulnerability data that helps security professionals identify and fix cyber threats. The NVD is maintained by the National Institute of Standards and Technology (NIST).

children.name: The Open Cloud Vulnerability & Security Issue Database
children.description: An open project to list all known cloud vulnerabilities and CSP security issues.

children.name: VulDB
children.description: Vulnarability database worldwide with more than 111 000 entries available.

children.name: Vulmon
children.description: Vulmon is a vulnerability search engine with vulnerability intelligence features. Vulmon conducts full text search in its database; therefore, you can search everything related to vulnerabilities. It includes CVE ID, vulnerability types, vendors, products, exploits, operating systems and anything else related to vulnerabilities.

children.name: Vulncheck
children.description: VulnCheck helps organizations outpace adversaries with vulnerability intelligence that predicts avenues of attack with speed and accuracy.
